calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Jewish Family & Children's Services.

The Court stated that there is an issue as to whether the statutory fees are adequate enough to cover the extraordinary fees requested. Mr. Feder stated that this is in regards to getting the copy of the lost will admitted, that the fee report supports the time spent, that extraordinary time was involved, that given the consequences of failing they went above and beyond, that if they had not been able to persuade the Court the money would have been escheated to the state, that there was much communication and review of records, that it had to do with the extent they went to, the time spent and the responsibility they had, that in regards to the numbers it was laid out in the declaration, that through yesterday the total fees are about $38,500.00, that they estimate there will be an additional $3-$7,000.00 to close out the estate, that both the extraordinary and statutory fees are reasonable, that the beneficiary has consented and was aware that they could have obtained their own counsel, that the estate was cash and securities, that it was not a complicated estate and that if not for the time spent admitting the copy of the lost will the statutory fees probably would have covered it. The Court took the petition for final distribution under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for (petitioner) Jewish Family & Children's Services, Daniel Hobach (senior officer) present.

The Court stated that this is for probate of a lost will, that we have a copy but no original will, that there is a question as to whether usual presumption does not apply or is rebutted and asked if counsel had anything new or different to report. Mr. Feder stated that there are two areas, that one is whether the conservatee was mentally competent until the end of life and that she was not, that the court investigator's reports for 2012 & 2014 stated that the conservatee was no longer capable of completing the voter registration form, that and amended declaration was filed this morning with attached medical reports that are revealing as to this issue, summarized reports from 2010 & 2011 regarding health status and asked that the court take judicial notice of the court investigators reports. Mr. Feder further stated that Mr. Margolin delivered the original will to Ms. Justh at the Jewish Home, that he visited her there on many occasions, that he advised her to call him if she needed revisions to the will, that Ms. Justh never raised questions regarding the will or expressed any unhappiness at or with the Jewish Home, that there is circumstantial evidence that the will was lost, that they know where the will was, that they have a copy of it, that Ms. Justh was under conservatorship, that the Jewish Home never received a call from another attorney or received an invoice from another attorney, that it is reasonable to suppose that she did not revoke her will, that she had no family as all was lost in the Holocaust, that she was involved in the jewish community, that there is no indication that there is anyone else that her estate should go to and that they have provided enough evidence. The petition for letters of administration with will annexed was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Continued To Oct-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Jewish Family & Children's Services (JFCS).

The Court stated that this is a hearing regarding a lost will, that the issue is if there is sufficient evidence that the will was destroyed and should not be enforced, that Mr. Margolin states that she asked for it back and that we do not know when or why she wanted it back. Mr. Feder stated that what we have is enough based on the long relationship between Ms. Justh and Mr. Margolin, that he would have been contacted if changes were wanted, that she was under a conservatorship and that the conservator provided a copy of the will to JFCS. Mr. Feder further stated that there is a declaration from Rebecca Paul, that the statute requires that Ms. Justh be competent until death, that the Court Investigator's report in 2010 indicated that she was conversational and still retained the right to vote, subsequent reports in 2012 & 2014 indicate that she is incapable of completing the voter registration. The Court stated that the timing of everything has not been presented to the court and is not in Mr. Margolin's declaration. Mr. Feder referred to 6124 regarding possible lack of competence from 2012-2015. The Court stated that if she had the will when she was competent she could have destroyed it, asked if Mr. Margolin had been asked why she asked for the will to be returned and that the declaration states that it was returned at her request. Mr. Feder stated that he does not know if Mr. Margolin had been asked why she wanted it returned, that in regards to the issue of capacity presumption does not apply and that the statute and presumption does not apply to someone who does not have capacity. The Court stated that it does not believe that the conservator tells us anything about the conservators collection of papers, how they were maintained or if a search was made. Mr. Feder stated that he could obtain a declaration and that they always make a thorough search. The petition for letters of administration with will annexed was continued to October 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for supplemental evidence to be filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Sep-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
